title: Lesson 32 – Advice & Probability
description: Ratschläge und Vermutungen: ～たほうがいいです (man sollte besser), ～かもしれません (vielleicht), ～でしょう (wahrscheinlich).

vocabulary.meaning: to rest, to take a day off
to sleep, to go to bed
to lose weight, to become thin

grammar.title: V た form + ほうが いいです (Had better ~)
grammar.structure: V た form + ほうが いいです / V ない form + ほうが いいです
grammar.explanation: This pattern is used to give advice or make a recommendation. ～たほうがいいです means 'you had better do ~' or 'it would be better to ~'. The negative form ～ないほうがいいです means 'you had better not ~'. The た form version suggests doing something, while the ない form advises against doing something.
grammar.example_sentences:
① 薬を 飲んだほうが いいですよ。
  (Kusuri o nonda hou ga ii desu yo.)
  — You had better take some medicine.
② 今日は 無理をしないほうが いいですよ。
  (Kyou wa muri o shinai hou ga ii desu yo.)
  — You had better not push yourself today.
③ もっと 野菜を 食べたほうが いいです。
  (Motto yasai o tabeta hou ga ii desu.)
  — You should eat more vegetables.

grammar.title: ～かもしれません (Might, maybe)
grammar.structure: V plain / い-Adj / な-Adj (な→) / N (な→) + かもしれません
grammar.explanation: ～かもしれません expresses the speaker's guess that something might be the case. It indicates a lower degree of certainty than でしょう. For な-adjectives and nouns, な is dropped before かもしれません. The casual form is かもしれない, often shortened to かも.
grammar.example_sentences:
④ 明日は 雨が 降るかもしれません。
  (Ashita wa ame ga furu kamoshiremasen.)
  — It might rain tomorrow.
⑤ あの レストランは 高いかもしれません。
  (Ano resutoran wa takai kamoshiremasen.)
  — That restaurant might be expensive.
⑥ 田中さんは 病気かもしれません。
  (Tanaka-san wa byouki kamoshiremasen.)
  — Mr. Tanaka might be sick.

grammar.title: ～でしょう (Probably, I think)
grammar.structure: V plain / い-Adj / な-Adj (な→) / N (な→) + でしょう
grammar.explanation: ～でしょう expresses the speaker's conjecture or forecast. It indicates that the speaker thinks something is probably the case. It is commonly used in weather forecasts. With a rising intonation, it can be used to confirm something. でしょう has a higher degree of certainty than かもしれません.
grammar.example_sentences:
⑦ 午後は 晴れるでしょう。
  (Gogo wa hareru deshou.)
  — It will probably be sunny in the afternoon.
⑧ 明日は 寒いでしょう。
  (Ashita wa samui deshou.)
  — It will probably be cold tomorrow.
⑨ 台風は 来ないでしょう。
  (Taifuu wa konai deshou.)
  — The typhoon probably won't come.

conversation.title: You had better rest
conversation.title_ja: 休んだほうが いいですよ

lines.speaker: Suzuki
lines.japanese: リーさん、顔色が 悪いですね。大丈夫ですか。
lines.romaji: Rii-san, kaoiro ga warui desu ne. Daijoubu desu ka.
lines.english: Lee, you don't look well. Are you okay?

lines.speaker: Lee
lines.japanese: ちょっと 熱が あるかもしれません。体が だるいです。
lines.romaji: Chotto netsu ga aru kamoshiremasen. Karada ga darui desu.
lines.english: I might have a slight fever. My body feels sluggish.

lines.speaker: Suzuki
lines.japanese: それは いけませんね。薬を 飲んだほうが いいですよ。
lines.romaji: Sore wa ikemasen ne. Kusuri o nonda hou ga ii desu yo.
lines.english: That's not good. You should take some medicine.

lines.speaker: Lee
lines.japanese: ええ、さっき 飲みました。でも、今日 会議が あるんです。
lines.romaji: Ee, sakki nomimashita. Demo, kyou kaigi ga arun desu.
lines.english: Yes, I just took some. But I have a meeting today.

lines.speaker: Suzuki
lines.japanese: 無理をしないほうが いいですよ。今日は 早く 帰って 休んだほうが いいです。
lines.romaji: Muri o shinai hou ga ii desu yo. Kyou wa hayaku kaette yasunda hou ga ii desu.
lines.english: You shouldn't push yourself. You should go home early and rest today.

lines.speaker: Lee
lines.japanese: そうですね。明日は 大丈夫でしょうか。
lines.romaji: Sou desu ne. Ashita wa daijoubu deshou ka.
lines.english: You're right. Will I be okay by tomorrow?

lines.speaker: Suzuki
lines.japanese: 十分 寝たら、たぶん よくなるでしょう。お大事に。
lines.romaji: Juubun netara, tabun yoku naru deshou. Odaiji ni.
lines.english: If you sleep well, you'll probably get better. Take care.

lines.speaker: Lee
lines.japanese: ありがとうございます。そうします。
